about.name: Sunya
about.quote:
"Life is a collection of fragments —
a sketch, a photograph, a place
that stays with you."
bio: I work with pencil, watercolor, and gouache — trying to catch the colors and small details that make everyday life worth looking at. I'm drawn to things that feel both peaceful and vibrant at the same time. hi bob

I admire Claude Monet for how he could make a haystack or a water lily feel monumental through light and color. Camille Pissarro too — his bright palettes somehow never feel forced; they belong exactly where he placed them.

These sketches, paintings, and photographs come from a notebook and a camera carried through different cities and seasons. Some are studied, others are stumbled upon. All of them are attempts to hold onto something worth looking at twice.

art.title: Portrait
art.description: I came across a photo of this actress on Instagram and was stopped by her expression — quiet, direct, yet gentle.

art.title: Ducks
art.description: I wanted to paint something in motion — the image of ducks gliding through a creek came to mind. Simple movement, reflected light, the small moments that get overlooked.

travel.caption: skyline
travel.description: After living in this city for a while, I stopped looking out the window. But this sunset — the colors were so vivid they pulled me right out of my routine.

travel.caption: wall collage
travel.description: The old retro-style posters covering the walls made the space feel more like a gallery than a restaurant. It became one of those small, unexpectedly memorable places.

travel.caption: wall of doodles
travel.description: While walking through a busy shopping street in Shanghai, I spotted a corner of a store covered in hand-drawn doodles of cats. It became the highlight of my trip.

travel.caption: Landscape
travel.description: After a long climb up the mountain, this was the first view that opened before me — the town below, the lake between, and the mountains far behind.

travel.caption: lakeview
travel.description: It was a busy day of sightseeing, but this lake had a stillness to it. A quiet pause in a full day — I liked the calmness of this view.

travel.caption: Skyline
travel.description: Across the water, the Hong Kong skyline felt calm and enormous at the same time. The buildings felt quiet instead.

travel.caption: River
travel.description: From the boat, I noticed this small cat sitting across the water, framed by red rails, plants, and old storefronts.

travel.caption: Skyline
travel.description: At night, Shenzhen turned into lines of color and light. The buildings felt almost unreal: sharp, bright, and futuristic.
